metadata.description: SmartPatch Network Controls — production-grade schema (NET-01 .. NET-11). Each control contains enforcement commands, validation checks, failure modes, privileges and authoritative references. Added sensible network controls (SMB encryption, host firewalling for SMB) based on scope/criteria.
metadata.author: SmartPatch Team (converted)

controls.name: Disable SMBv1 (client & server)
controls.description: Remove/disable legacy SMBv1 server and client components to eliminate old, wormable protocol surface.
controls.rationale: SMBv1 is deprecated, insecure and commonly abused by ransomware/worms. Modern Windows supports SMBv2/3 — remove SMBv1 where possible.

os_applicability.notes: Older images may not expose SMB1 as a removable feature; test and stage.
SMBv1 can be disabled via optional feature / DISM / Set-SmbServerConfiguration.

enforcement_methods.notes: Use Set-SmbServerConfiguration / Set-SmbClientConfiguration and confirm with Get-SmbServerConfiguration / Get-SmbClientConfiguration.
DISM / OptionalFeatures remove SMB1 feature components.

enforcement_methods.description: Deploy registry/DISM changes via Configuration Manager or a GPO-backed preference script for broad rollout and reporting.

validation_checks.command: Get-SmbServerConfiguration | Select-Object -Property EnableSMB1Protocol
validation_checks.success_criteria: EnableSMB1Protocol equals False.

validation_checks.command: Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName SMB1Protocol*
validation_checks.success_criteria: Feature state is Disabled/NotPresent where expected.

validation_checks.command: Attempt to negotiate SMB1 (lab tooling) or run vulnerability scanner
validation_checks.success_criteria: No SMBv1 connections accepted.

failure_modes: Legacy appliances or backup devices that only support SMBv1 will fail — plan vendor migration or isolation.
Some OS builds may report feature removed but backend server config may still allow SMB1 until a reboot or further change.

priority.priority_reason: SMBv1 is a high-risk, widely exploited legacy protocol; industry baselines mandate removal.

references.title: Detect, enable, and disable SMBv1, SMBv2, and SMBv3 in Windows
Use Configuration Manager / baseline to disable SMBv1 at scale
CIS guidance: disable SMBv1

controls.implementation_notes: Pilot on non-critical servers, inventory SMB dependencies (NAS, printers, scanner appliances). After removal, verify backup/restore paths.

controls.name: Require SMB signing (server) / enable client signing
controls.description: Ensure SMB sessions are signed to protect against man-in-the-middle tampering and session spoofing.
controls.rationale: SMB signing provides message integrity for SMB traffic; requiring signing prevents unauthenticated tampering. For SMB2/3 this uses stronger algorithms than SMB1 signing.

os_applicability.notes: Align server and client settings; test interoperability with non-Windows SMB targets (NAS).

enforcement_methods.notes: Set-SmbServerConfiguration changes server-side signing/encryption options. Confirm via Get-SmbServerConfiguration.
Set client to require signing where compatible.

enforcement_methods.description: Use 'Microsoft network client: Digitally sign communications (always)' and 'Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (always)' in Local Policies → Security Options.

validation_checks.command: Get-SmbServerConfiguration | Select RequireSecuritySignature,EnableSecuritySignature
validation_checks.success_criteria: RequireSecuritySignature = True and/or EnableSecuritySignature = True as policy requires.

validation_checks.command: Get-SmbClientConfiguration | Select RequireSecuritySignature,EnableSecuritySignature
validation_checks.success_criteria: Client set to Require/Enable signing if mandated.

validation_checks.command: Capture SMB negotiation and confirm signing flag negotiated; or run vulnerability scanner
validation_checks.success_criteria: SMB connections are signed; scanner no longer flags 'SMB signing not required'.

failure_modes: Enforcing signing can break interoperability with older SMB/third-party devices that don't support signing; may reduce throughput on very busy file servers.
Misapplied settings can cause denied connections — stage and monitor.

priority.priority_reason: Prevents SMB MITM/downgrade attacks and aligns with STIG/CIS recommendations.

audit_query_examples: Check STIG/CIS checks for 'Digitally sign communications (always)'.

references.title: SMB signing overview and guidance
STIG: Digitally sign communications (always) enforcement example
Community guidance & remediation examples (MS Q&A)

controls.implementation_notes: Inventory devices lacking signing support, plan exceptions or isolate them. Monitor performance; enabling signing may increase CPU usage on older NICs/hosts.

controls.name: Disable NetBIOS over TCP/IP (NetBT)
controls.description: Disable NetBIOS over TCP/IP to reduce legacy name resolution and broadcast-based enumeration vectors.
controls.rationale: NetBIOS/NetBT is legacy, increases broadcast surface and is frequently used in local enumeration attacks; modern DNS should be used instead.

os_applicability.notes: Per-adapter setting; can be controlled via DHCP option or registry per NIC interface.

commands: For each interface GUID under HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NetBT\Parameters\Interfaces:\ Set NetbiosOptions (REG_DWORD) = 2

PowerShell example (per host):
Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_NetworkAdapterConfiguration | Where-Object {$_.TcpipNetbiosOptions -ne $null} | Invoke-CimMethod -MethodName SetTcpipNetbios -Arguments @{TcpipNetbiosOptions=2}
enforcement_methods.notes: Value meanings: 0 = use DHCP, 1 = enable, 2 = disable.

enforcement_methods.description: Use DHCP vendor option (001 Microsoft Disable NetBIOS Option) to instruct clients to disable NetBIOS.

validation_checks.command: Query HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NetBT\Parameters\Interfaces\<GUID> for NetbiosOptions
validation_checks.success_criteria: NetbiosOptions equals 2 for managed adapters (or DHCP instructs disable).

validation_checks.command: nbtstat -n and nbtstat -c to confirm no NetBIOS names registered
validation_checks.success_criteria: No active NetBIOS names / broadcasts suppressed.

failure_modes: Legacy apps or devices that depend on NetBIOS/WINS may fail; coordinate with app owners and test docking stations or USB network adapters.
Per-adapter setting can result in inconsistent state if new NICs appear dynamically.

priority.priority_reason: Removes legacy broadcast-based enumeration surface; low operational impact when DNS is fully functional.

references.title: NetbiosOptions (Unattend / registry) and disabling NetBIOS guidance
How to disable NetBIOS over TCP/IP (DHCP & registry)

controls.implementation_notes: Prefer DHCP-based disable for scale; otherwise inventory adapters and deploy per-interface registry changes with monitoring for new NICs.

controls.name: Disable LLMNR (Turn off multicast name resolution)
controls.description: Disable LLMNR to remove a local network name-resolution protocol vulnerable to poisoning and credential capture.
controls.rationale: LLMNR/NBT-NS are easily spoofed on local networks; disabling reduces credential-theft attack surface.

os_applicability.notes: Disable via Group Policy or registry policy; also disable NBT-NS/NetBIOS where feasible.

enforcement_methods.description: Enable 'Turn Off Multicast Name Resolution' at Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Network → DNS Client.
enforcement_methods.notes: This sets the related policy/key under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\DNSClient.
Value 0 (or policy enabled) disables multicast name resolution in managed environments.

validation_checks.command: reg query "HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\DNSClient" /v EnableMulticast
validation_checks.success_criteria: EnableMulticast exists and equals 0 or GPO shows 'Turn Off Multicast Name Resolution' enabled.

validation_checks.command: Attempt LLMNR name resolution in an isolated lab (e.g., by sending an LLMNR query) and verify no response from managed hosts.
validation_checks.success_criteria: No LLMNR responses from endpoints with policy applied.

failure_modes: Some extremely old or misconfigured networks may depend on LLMNR; test pilot first.
Disabling LLMNR does not affect DNS; if DNS is misconfigured some name resolution may fail and cause user impact.
controls.functional_impact: Low (tests recommend no observed breakage in modern environments) — verify exceptions.

priority.priority_reason: LLMNR poisoning is a common lateral-movement enabler; disabling is a low-cost mitigation.

audit_query_examples: GPO: Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Network → DNS Client → Turn off multicast name resolution

references.title: Turn off multicast name resolution (disable LLMNR) — Group Policy & registry guidance
LLMNR poisoning risks and remediation examples

controls.implementation_notes: Deploy via GPO in Audit first (log-only) if you have concerns, then Enforce. Also disable NetBIOS (NET-03) for maximal reduction in broadcast-based name resolution.

controls.name: Disable WPAD / Auto Proxy Discovery
controls.description: Disable Web Proxy Auto-Discovery (WPAD) and related auto-proxy mechanisms to prevent WPAD hijacking and proxy poisoning.
controls.rationale: WPAD is frequently abused to force clients to use attacker-controlled proxies; disabling reduces exposure.

os_applicability.notes: Multiple components (WinHTTP service, browser auto-detect settings) can trigger WPAD; disable all relevant mechanisms.

enforcement_methods.description: Turn off 'Automatically detect settings' under Settings → Network & Internet → Proxy (affects many apps/browsers).

commands: sc config WinHttpAutoProxySvc start= disabled & sc stop WinHttpAutoProxySvc
enforcement_methods.notes: Use DisableWpad registry key to disable WPAD detection while leaving the WinHTTP service in expected state; Microsoft docs provide guidance for full disable steps.

enforcement_methods.description: Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Windows Components → Internet Explorer / Edge: disable automatic proxy detection and block changing automatic configuration settings.

validation_checks.command: reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\WinHttp" /v DisableWpad & sc query WinHttpAutoProxySvc
validation_checks.success_criteria: DisableWpad = 1 and WinHttpAutoProxySvc stopped/disabled (or per MS guidance).

validation_checks.command: Verify that automatic WPAD queries are not issued (e.g., using network capture or proxy logs).
validation_checks.success_criteria: No WPAD DNS/DHCP client lookups from endpoints with policy applied.

failure_modes: Some enterprise software relying on automatic proxy discovery may break — coordinate with network/proxy owners and provide manual proxy configuration where required.
Disabling the service incorrectly (instead of registry flags) can break WinHTTP-dependent components — follow Microsoft guidance.

priority.priority_reason: WPAD attacks are high-impact; disabling is fairly low-friction with proper inventory.

audit_query_examples: Check proxy automatic-detect UI setting and network captures for WPAD queries

references.title: How to disable WPAD and WinHTTP auto-proxy features (Microsoft troubleshooting guidance)
Correct registry approach to disable WPAD (practical guidance)

controls.implementation_notes: Document a fallback manual proxy configuration plan for users and test critical WinHTTP-dependent apps before mass rollout.

controls.name: Restrict RDP to trusted networks (host firewall / allow-list)
controls.description: Limit RDP exposure by restricting inbound RDP access to internal/trusted IP ranges or specific jump hosts (use RD Gateway where possible).
controls.rationale: Exposed RDP is a common initial access vector. Host-based firewall rules that limit remote addresses reduce attack surface.

os_applicability.notes: Host firewall rules apply across supported Windows versions; consider RD Gateway for remote access needs.

commands: New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "Allow RDP from LAN" -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 3389 -RemoteAddress LocalSubnet -Action Allow
New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "Block RDP from Internet" -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 3389 -RemoteAddress Any -Profile Public -Action Block
enforcement_methods.notes: Implement allow-list rule(s) for trusted IP ranges, then ensure no broad 'Allow' rule exists for public profiles. Use RD Gateway / VPN instead of direct Internet exposure.

enforcement_methods.description: Deploy firewall rule sets via Group Policy or endpoint management for consistent application.

validation_checks.command: Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "Allow RDP from LAN","Block RDP from Internet" | Get-NetFirewallAddressFilter
validation_checks.success_criteria: Allow rule restricts RemoteAddress=LocalSubnet (or specified ranges); Block rule covers public profiles.

validation_checks.command: Attempt RDP from an untrusted external IP (lab) and verify blocked; test from a trusted IP to verify allowed.
validation_checks.success_criteria: Untrusted access blocked; trusted allowed via allowed path only (RDG/VPN).

failure_modes: Remote administrators may lose direct access — ensure RD Gateway/VPN management paths and break-glass procedures.
Incorrect rule scoping may accidentally block domain-joined management tools.
controls.functional_impact: Medium — affects remote admin access and users relying on direct RDP.

priority.priority_reason: RDP exposure is a frequent exploitation vector; host-level restrictions are effective and pragmatic.

references.title: New-NetFirewallRule cmdlet documentation (create firewall rules / RemoteAddress/LocalSubnet usage)
How to restrict RDP to specific IP addresses (operational guidance)

controls.implementation_notes: Prefer RD Gateway + MFA for remote admin; implement allow-lists rather than wide-open blocking rules where possible.

controls.name: Restrict ICMP (Echo) responses via host firewall
controls.description: Limit or block ICMP Echo (ping) responses on endpoints where not required to reduce reconnaissance surface.
controls.rationale: Blocking ICMP Echo reduces simple network scanning and discovery by adversaries; allow only where monitoring depends on ping.

os_applicability.notes: Host firewall controls ICMP rules; some server roles may need ICMP enabled for monitoring.

commands: New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "Block ICMP Echo Request" -Direction Inbound -Protocol ICMPv4 -IcmpType 8 -Action Block -Profile Any
enforcement_methods.notes: Alternatively enable/disable the predefined 'File and Printer Sharing (Echo Request - ICMPv4-In)' rules.

validation_checks.command: Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName 'Block ICMP Echo Request' | Get-NetFirewallPortFilter
validation_checks.success_criteria: Rule exists and is Enabled/Action=Block for ICMP type 8.

validation_checks.command: From a test host, run Test-Connection -Count 3 <target> and observe behavior
validation_checks.success_criteria: Ping fails from unallowed hosts; allowed monitoring hosts succeed if permitted.

failure_modes: Some management/monitoring solutions rely on ICMP; coordinate with monitoring teams to create allow-lists.
Network devices or appliances that respond to ICMP may be unaffected by host firewall rules.
controls.functional_impact: Low by default; medium if monitoring depends on ICMP.

priority.priority_reason: Reduces easy reconnaissance with minimal operational cost in many environments.

references.title: Windows firewall examples for ICMP / File and Printer Sharing (Echo Request) rules
Enable/disable firewall rules and manage ICMP with PowerShell (operational docs)

controls.implementation_notes: Pilot with monitoring to ensure no alerting gaps. Use host firewall logging to detect blocked reconnaissance attempts.

controls.name: Enforce SMB client signing / client settings
controls.description: Configure SMB client-side signing settings to align with server-side signing enforcement and to refuse unsigned sessions where required.
controls.rationale: Client-side enforcement prevents accidental connections to servers that do not sign SMB sessions and avoids downgrade exposure.

os_applicability.notes: Client settings may prevent some legacy connections; plan exceptions.

commands: Or configure via Group Policy: 'Microsoft network client: Digitally sign communications (always)'.

validation_checks.command: Get-SmbClientConfiguration | Select RequireSecuritySignature,EnableSecuritySignature
validation_checks.success_criteria: RequireSecuritySignature = True (if policy requires).

validation_checks.command: Attempt SMB connection to a non-signed server to validate failure if RequireSecuritySignature is True.
validation_checks.success_criteria: Unsigned connections refused when policy applied.

failure_modes: Non-Windows SMB servers (older NAS) may not support client signing — create exception handling or network segmentation.
Client-side strict settings may break legacy automation and mount scripts.
controls.functional_impact: Medium — may break legacy client-to-server mounts.

priority.priority_reason: Complements server-side signing enforcement to provide defense-in-depth.

audit_query_examples: Attempt connection tests to representative servers

references.title: SMB signing overview & examples; client/server configuration

controls.implementation_notes: Staged rollout: inventory client SMB usage, pilot on controlled groups, then expand. Provide exception list for unmanaged NAS.

controls.name: Disable SMB null sessions / restrict anonymous access
controls.description: Prevent anonymous SMB (null session) enumeration by enabling RestrictNullSessAccess and related security options.
controls.rationale: Null sessions allow attackers to enumerate users/shares; restricting anonymous access closes this vector.

os_applicability.notes: Applies to servers and domain controllers; confirm registry/policy enforced on DCs.

commands: Configure Local Policy: Network access: Restrict anonymous access to Named Pipes and Shares = Enabled
enforcement_methods.notes: Also ensure 'Network access: Shares that can be accessed anonymously' and 'Named pipes that can be accessed anonymously' are empty.

enforcement_methods.description: Deploy the above security options via GPO (Computer Configuration → Windows Settings → Security Settings → Local Policies → Security Options).

validation_checks.command: reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters" /v RestrictNullSessAccess
validation_checks.success_criteria: RestrictNullSessAccess equals 1 (REG_DWORD).

validation_checks.command: Run null session enumeration tools in a lab to confirm access is blocked; review security logs for attempted anonymous connections.
validation_checks.success_criteria: Anonymous enumeration attempts fail; scanners no longer detect 'null session' exposure.

failure_modes: Misconfigured share ACLs or policy gaps may leave indirect anonymous access vectors open; confirm related 'anonymous' settings are blank/disabled.
Some legacy services expecting anonymous SMB may stop working — identify exceptions.
controls.functional_impact: Low–Medium depending on legacy anonymous requirements.

priority.priority_reason: Null sessions are a direct information-disclosure vector used during recon and privilege escalation.

audit_query_examples: Verify 'Network access: Shares that can be accessed anonymously' is blank

references.title: Network access: Restrict anonymous access to Named Pipes and Shares (registry/policy)

controls.implementation_notes: Apply to DCs and file servers first; run authenticated scans after change to ensure no residual anonymous ACLs exist.

controls.name: Enforce SMB encryption (server & share-level)
controls.description: Enable SMB encryption on servers or per-share to protect SMB traffic privacy without requiring network-level tunnels.
controls.rationale: SMB 3.x supports native encryption; enabling it protects data-in-flight and reduces need to rely solely on network encryption.

os_applicability.notes: Clients must support SMB3 encryption (Windows 8+/Server 2012+ or modern Samba). Older clients will fail to connect if encryption required.

enforcement_methods.notes: This forces encryption on server-side; verify client compatibility.
Use share-level encryption if you need selective protection.
RequireEncryption on client ensures outbound connections use encryption or fail if unsupported.

validation_checks.command: Get-SmbServerConfiguration | Select EncryptData
validation_checks.success_criteria: EncryptData = True for server-wide enforcement or per-share EncryptData true.

validation_checks.command: Connect from a modern client and verify with Get-SmbConnection that Encryption is in use; attempt connection from legacy client to confirm failure behavior (lab).
validation_checks.success_criteria: Encrypted SMB sessions are established; legacy clients fail (document exceptions).

failure_modes: Legacy clients (pre-SMB3) will be unable to connect if server requires encryption; plan exceptions or selective share encryption.
Performance impact on some workloads; test throughput and CPU on file servers.

priority.priority_reason: Encrypts SMB traffic and reduces risk of data interception on untrusted networks.

references.title: Set-SmbServerConfiguration examples (turn on signing and encryption)
SMB security enhancements and encryption guidance
Configure SMB client to require encryption

controls.implementation_notes: Prefer per-share encryption for mixed-client environments, or pilot server-wide encryption after verifying client compatibility.

controls.name: Block inbound SMB from untrusted networks (host firewall)
controls.description: Prevent inbound SMB (TCP/445, TCP/139) from untrusted or internet networks at host firewall level; allow only internal subnets or management VLANs.
controls.rationale: Exposed SMB ports are high-value attack surface; firewall-based block minimizes lateral movement and Internet-exposed file shares.

os_applicability.notes: Host firewall rules are applicable across Windows family; enforce via GPO or endpoint management.

commands: New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "Block SMB inbound from Untrusted" -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 445,139 -RemoteAddress Any -Profile Public -Action Block
New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "Allow SMB inbound from LAN" -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 445,139 -RemoteAddress LocalSubnet -Action Allow
enforcement_methods.notes: Implement allow-on-LAN then block other profiles; coordinate with network-level firewall policies to avoid conflicts.

enforcement_methods.description: Ensure network perimeter firewalls block SMB (TCP 445/139) from Internet to internal networks as additional layer.

validation_checks.command: Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName 'Block SMB inbound from Untrusted','Allow SMB inbound from LAN' | Get-NetFirewallAddressFilter
validation_checks.success_criteria: Rules exist and scope matches intended RemoteAddress and profiles.

validation_checks.command: Attempt to connect to SMB from an external test host (lab) and confirm blocked; test internal connectivity remains.
validation_checks.success_criteria: External SMB attempts blocked; internal allowed per scope.

failure_modes: Incorrect scoping may block legitimate cross-subnet file replication or backup jobs — inventory required.
Relying solely on host firewall is insufficient; use layered controls (network firewall, segmentation).

priority.priority_reason: Blocking SMB from untrusted networks is a straightforward, high-impact mitigation.

audit_query_examples: Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName 'Block SMB inbound from Untrusted' | Get-NetFirewallAddressFilter

references.title: Manage Windows Firewall with the command line (netsh / PowerShell examples)
SMB port/usage & securing SMB (operational guidance)

controls.implementation_notes: Combine host firewall rules with perimeter filtering and network segmentation (file server VLANs). Maintain exception list for backup/replication endpoints.

source_references_loadbearing.claim: SMBv1 should be disabled; use Microsoft guidance for detection and removal.
SMB signing can/should be required via server/client config or GPO (Registry and Set-SmbServerConfiguration).
NetBIOS disable uses NetbiosOptions = 2 (per-interface registry) or DHCP vendor option.
LLMNR disabled via 'Turn off multicast name resolution' GPO / EnableMulticast registry key.
WPAD must be disabled via registry/WinHTTP guidance and UI settings; follow Microsoft troubleshooting doc.
